steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: This is a word-in-context question. The blank takes the economist's projection as its object.

steps.title: Use the contrast structure
steps.content: "Unlikely to ______ her projection entirely BUT will almost certainly extend its time frame" sets up a contrast: the delays will not totally nullify the projection — they will only postpone it. The blank needs a word meaning "nullify" or "render void."

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'dispute': to dispute is to argue against, which is something a person does, not something supply-chain delays do. Delays cannot debate a projection. ✗
  • Choice B — 'withdraw': only the economist who made the projection could withdraw it; delays cannot withdraw someone else's claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'underscore': means to emphasize or reinforce, the opposite of what the contrast ("unlikely to ___ entirely BUT") requires. ✗
That matches choice D — D is correct.
